question_text: A kettle and a toaster are both plugged into a 230 V mains circuit in parallel. The kettle has a resistance of 23 Ω and the toaster has a resistance of 46 Ω. Which appliance draws more current, and why?

options.label: A
options.text: The toaster draws more current because it has higher resistance

options.label: B
options.text: The kettle draws more current because it has lower resistance and both have the same voltage across them

options.label: C
options.text: Both draw the same current because they are plugged into mains voltage

options.label: D
options.text: The toaster draws more current because parallel circuits share current equally

explanation: Using I = V ÷ R: kettle current = 230 ÷ 23 = 10 A; toaster current = 230 ÷ 46 = 5 A. In parallel circuits, both devices experience the same voltage (230 V), but the lower resistance appliance draws more current. This is why kettles use more electricity than toasters.
